name: Port of Shanghai
note: World's largest container port by throughput; Yangshan deepwater terminal

name: Port of Ningbo-Zhoushan
note: World's largest port by total cargo tonnage; iron ore and crude gateway

name: Port of Singapore
note: World's largest transshipment hub; controls Malacca Strait throughput

name: Port of Rotterdam
note: Europe's largest port; primary crude oil and LNG gateway

name: Port of Antwerp-Bruges
note: Second largest European port; dominant chemicals hub; major LNG bunkering

name: Port of Los Angeles / Long Beach
note: Largest US port complex; ~40% of US container imports; key Asia-Americas link

name: Port of Houston
note: Largest US port by foreign tonnage; dominates US petrochemical exports

name: Port of Santos
note: Latin America's largest port; critical soybean and sugar export gateway

name: Port Hedland
note: World's largest bulk export port; ~20% of global seaborne iron ore

name: Port of Saldanha Bay
note: Africa's largest iron ore export terminal; feeds Asian steel mills

name: Port of Tubarao (Vitoria)
note: Vale's primary iron ore export terminal; one of largest bulk terminals in Americas

name: Port of Jebel Ali
note: World's largest man-made port; dominant Middle East / South Asia transshipment hub

name: Port of Busan
note: Northeast Asia's largest transshipment hub; Korea-Japan-China feeder network

name: Port of Guangzhou (Nansha)
note: Pearl River Delta's primary container gateway; third largest Chinese port

name: Port of Tianjin (Xingang)
note: Gateway to Beijing and North China industrial corridor; major auto hub

name: Jamnagar Refinery Complex (Reliance)
note: World's largest single-site refining complex; processes many crudes for export

name: Ras Tanura Refinery
note: World's largest offshore oil loading facility; strategic Saudi export terminal

name: Port Arthur Refinery (Motiva)
note: Largest single US refinery; heavy crude optimized; ties US Gulf to Saudi supply

name: Galveston Bay Refinery (Marathon)
note: Second largest US refinery; heavy sour crude specialist; major gasoline supplier

name: Pernis Refinery (Shell)
note: Europe's largest refinery; price-setting role in NW European crude benchmarking

name: Ulsan Refinery (SK Energy)
note: Northeast Asia's largest refining hub; key jet fuel and naphtha supplier

name: Jurong Island Refinery Cluster
note: Southeast Asia's primary refining cluster; benchmark for Asia Pacific distillate pricing

name: Paraguana Refinery Complex (CRP)
note: Largest refinery in Western Hemisphere by nameplate; underutilized due to sanctions

name: Ruwais Refinery (ADNOC)
note: Middle East's largest single-site refinery; positions UAE as refined products exporter

name: Ras Laffan LNG Complex
note: World's largest LNG export facility; North Field underpins Qatar's dominance

name: Sabine Pass LNG Terminal
note: First major US LNG export terminal; key driver of US gas price linkage to global markets

name: Cameron LNG Terminal
note: Major US Gulf Coast LNG export facility; French and Japanese offtake

name: Corpus Christi LNG Terminal
note: Growing US export hub; stage 3 expansion; key European market supplier post-2022

name: Gladstone LNG Hub
note: Australia's east coast LNG hub; supplies Japan/Korea/China from coal-seam gas

name: Yamal LNG Terminal (Sabetta)
note: Arctic LNG flagship; pioneered ice-class LNG shipping; Asian/European swing factor

name: Bintulu LNG Complex (MLNG)
note: Malaysia's only LNG export hub; among world's oldest LNG complexes still expanding

name: Darwin LNG / Ichthys LNG
note: Key Japan-oriented LNG supply; Ichthys among world's largest gas condensate fields

name: Freeport LNG Terminal
note: Major US Gulf LNG hub; 2022 fire caused months-long disruption, exposed Europe fragility

name: Gorgon LNG (Barrow Island)
note: Australia's largest single LNG project; Gorgon and Jansz-Io fields; supplies Japan

name: Wheatstone LNG
note: Western Australian LNG hub feeding Japanese and Chinese long-term contracts

name: Guixi Copper Smelter (Jiangxi Copper)
note: World's largest single copper smelting complex; sets tone for Chinese TC/RC

name: Aurubis Hamburg Copper Smelter
note: Europe's largest copper smelter and world's largest copper recycler

name: Ventanas Smelter (Codelco)
note: Chile's primary copper smelting corridor; Codelco is world's largest copper producer

name: Almalyk Copper Smelter
note: Central Asia's largest copper complex; growing export role

name: EGA Al Taweelah Aluminum Smelter
note: World's largest single-site aluminum smelter outside China

name: Shandong Aluminum Smelter Cluster
note: World's dominant aluminum smelting region; Chinese overcapacity sets LME price ceiling

name: RUSAL Siberian Smelter Cluster
note: World's second largest aluminum producer; hydropower-based; sanction-sensitive

name: TSMC Hsinchu Science Park Fabs
note: TSMC's historic R&D and advanced-node core; national security asset

name: TSMC Fab 18 (N3/N2) - Tainan
note: World's only mass-production 3nm-2nm fab; Apple/NVIDIA/AMD depend on this site

name: TSMC Arizona Fab (Phoenix)
note: US geopolitical hedge fab; only advanced-node logic outside Asia; CHIPS-subsidized

name: Samsung Hwaseong Campus
note: Samsung's primary advanced logic and memory campus; 3nm GAA production

name: Samsung Pyeongtaek Campus
note: World's largest semiconductor campus by footprint; DRAM+NAND+foundry

name: SK Hynix Icheon Campus
note: World's leading HBM and DDR5 site; sole mass producer of HBM3E for AI accelerators

name: Intel Ocotillo Campus
note: Intel's most advanced US fab cluster; IDM 2.0 flagship; CHIPS recipient

name: Intel Ohio Fab (New Albany)
note: US greenfield advanced-node site; largest private semiconductor investment in US history

name: Micron Boise Fab
note: Only US-headquartered DRAM producer; Boise is global HQ and key memory R&D

name: ASML Veldhoven (EUV/DUV)
note: World's ONLY EUV lithography producer; single chokepoint for all sub-7nm chips globally

name: GlobalFoundries Dresden Fab 1
note: Europe's most advanced logic foundry; dominant automotive/RF chips; EU sovereign anchor

name: Micron Hiroshima DRAM Fab
note: Micron's largest overseas fab; key advanced DRAM node; Japan-subsidized expansion

name: SK Hynix Yongin Campus
note: SK Hynix's next-gen mega-campus; $95B over 20 years; critical AI memory supply

name: Port Klang (Westports)
note: Malaysia's largest port; major Southeast Asian transshipment hub

name: Port of Kaohsiung
note: Taiwan's primary container port; key link for semiconductor and electronics exports

name: Port of Colombo
note: Indian Ocean's primary transshipment hub; geopolitically contested China/India

name: Port of Abidjan
note: West Africa's largest port and cocoa export gateway; serves landlocked Sahel

name: Port of Durban
note: Africa's busiest container port; primary gateway for sub-Saharan imports and coal

name: Manifa Field
note: Manifa is one of the world's largest offshore heavy crude fields, developed via artificial islands in the Persian Gulf.

name: Shaybah Field
note: Shaybah produces light Arab Extra Light crude from the remote Empty Quarter desert near the UAE border.

name: Khurais Field
note: Khurais is a giant onshore Saudi field producing Arab Light crude with large-scale water-injection infrastructure.

name: Abqaiq Field
note: Abqaiq is a mature giant field whose adjacent plant is the world's largest crude stabilization facility.

name: Zuluf Field
note: Zuluf is a major offshore Saudi field undergoing expansion to add capacity of medium Arab crude.

name: Upper Zakum Field
note: Upper Zakum is one of the largest offshore fields in the world, developed via engineered artificial islands.

name: Bab Field (Murban)
note: The Bab field is a cornerstone of ADNOC's onshore output and feeds the Murban benchmark crude grade.

name: Bu Hasa Field
note: Bu Hasa is one of ADNOC's largest onshore fields producing light Murban-grade crude.

name: West Qurna-1 Field
note: West Qurna-1 is a supergiant southern Iraqi field producing Basra crude for export through the Gulf.

name: West Qurna-2 Field
note: West Qurna-2 is a Lukoil-operated supergiant field and one of Iraq's largest sources of crude output.

name: Majnoon Field
note: Majnoon is a giant field near the Iranian border that the Iraqi state operator runs after IOC handover.

name: Zubair Field
note: The Zubair field is a southern Iraqi giant operated under a technical service contract led by Eni.

name: Gachsaran Field
note: Gachsaran is one of Iran's largest onshore fields, producing in the Zagros foothills since the 1930s.

name: Marun Field
note: Marun is a supergiant Iranian field in Khuzestan and a major contributor to Iran's crude production.

name: Azadegan Field
note: Azadegan is a giant field on the Iran-Iraq border being developed in phases by Iranian operators.

name: Al-Shaheen Field
note: Al-Shaheen is Qatar's largest offshore oil field, operated by a TotalEnergies-QatarEnergy venture.

name: Raudhatain Field
note: Raudhatain in northern Kuwait is a major field central to KOC's plan to lift national capacity.

name: Wafra Field
note: Wafra is an onshore field in the Saudi-Kuwaiti Neutral Zone producing heavy crude jointly.

name: Sharara Field
note: Sharara is Libya's largest oil field, in the southwestern Murzuq Basin and prone to political disruption.

name: El Feel (Elephant) Field
note: El Feel is a southwestern Libyan field operated by an NOC-Eni joint venture in the Murzuq Basin.

name: Waha Fields (Sirte Basin)
note: The Waha concession in the Sirte Basin produces light crude exported via the Es Sider terminal.

name: Hassi Messaoud Field
note: Hassi Messaoud is Algeria's largest oil field and the heart of Saharan light Sahara Blend crude.

name: Hassi Berkine Field
note: Hassi Berkine in the eastern Berkine Basin is a major Algerian field developed with foreign partners.

name: Agbami Field
note: Agbami is a deepwater Nigerian field producing light sweet crude via a large FPSO offshore the Niger Delta.

name: Egina Field
note: Egina is a deepwater Nigerian field offshore the Niger Delta produced through a major FPSO.

name: Akpo Field
note: Akpo is a deepwater condensate and light-crude field in Nigeria's offshore OML 130 block.

name: Girassol Field (Block 17)
note: Girassol in deepwater Block 17 was the anchor field of Angola's offshore oil expansion.

name: Dalia Field (Block 17)
note: Dalia is a deepwater Angolan field in Block 17 produced through a large FPSO offshore Luanda.

name: Kaombo Field (Block 32)
note: Kaombo is an ultra-deepwater Angolan development in Block 32 produced by two converted FPSOs.

name: Jubilee Field
note: Jubilee is Ghana's flagship deepwater field offshore the western coast, producing light sweet crude.

name: Baleine Field
note: Baleine is a major deepwater discovery offshore Ivory Coast being developed by Eni in phases.

name: Buzios Field (pre-salt)
note: Buzios is the world's largest deepwater field, a pre-salt giant in Brazil's Santos Basin.

name: Tupi Field (Lula, pre-salt)
note: Tupi (formerly Lula) was the discovery that opened Brazil's prolific Santos Basin pre-salt play.

name: Sapinhoa Field (pre-salt)
note: Sapinhoa is a pre-salt field in the Santos Basin produced via FPSOs sharing infrastructure with Tupi.

name: Marlim Field
note: Marlim is a mature deepwater Campos Basin field undergoing a large redevelopment with new FPSOs.

name: Liza Field (Stabroek-adjacent)
note: Liza is the first producing development offshore Guyana, feeding the Destiny and Unity FPSOs.

name: Payara Field
note: Payara is the third major Guyana development produced through the Prosperity FPSO offshore.

name: Sangomar Field
note: Sangomar is Senegal's first offshore oil development, producing crude via an FPSO from 2024.

name: Kalamkas-Sea Field
note: Kalamkas-Sea is a Caspian offshore field being developed alongside the giant Kashagan project.

name: Karachaganak Field
note: Karachaganak is a giant condensate and gas field in northwest Kazakhstan exported via the CPC pipeline.

name: Romashkino Field
note: Romashkino in Tatarstan is one of the oldest supergiant fields of the Volga-Urals oil province.

name: Vankor Field
note: Vankor is a major East Siberian field and the core of Rosneft's Vostok Oil northern cluster.

name: Prudhoe Bay Field
note: Prudhoe Bay is the largest US oil field by reserves, feeding the Trans-Alaska Pipeline System.

name: Spraberry Trend (Permian)
note: The Spraberry trend is a vast Permian tight-oil play and among the highest-output US producing areas.

name: Thunder Horse Field
note: Thunder Horse is a major deepwater Gulf of Mexico field produced via a large semisubmersible platform.

name: Mars-Ursa Complex
note: The Mars-Ursa complex is a long-life deepwater Gulf of Mexico hub producing the Mars sour crude blend.

name: Daqing Field
note: Daqing in northeast China has been the country's largest oil field since the 1960s, now in deep decline management.

name: Shengli Field
note: Shengli in the Yellow River delta is China's second-largest oil field, operated by Sinopec.

name: Changqing Field
note: Changqing in the Ordos Basin is China's largest oil and gas field by combined output.

name: Penglai Field (Bohai Bay)
note: Penglai is the largest offshore field in China's Bohai Bay, operated by CNOOC.

name: Orinoco Belt - Carabobo Block
note: The Carabobo block in the Orinoco Belt holds vast extra-heavy crude reserves upgraded for export.

name: Orinoco Belt - Junin Block
note: The Junin block is part of Venezuela's Orinoco extra-heavy crude belt, the world's largest oil accumulation.

name: Cano Limon Field
note: Cano Limon is a major Colombian field in the eastern Llanos exporting via a pipeline to the Caribbean.

name: Rubiales Field
note: Rubiales in the Llanos Basin is Colombia's largest oil field, producing heavy crude.

name: Sacha Field
note: Sacha is one of Ecuador's largest fields in the Amazonian Oriente Basin, exported via the SOTE pipeline.

name: Rabi-Kounga Field
note: Rabi-Kounga was Gabon's largest onshore field, producing light crude in the coastal basin.

name: Forties Field
note: Forties is a mature central North Sea field that gives its name to the Forties crude price benchmark.

name: Buzzard Field
note: Buzzard is the largest UK North Sea field and a major component of the dated Brent Forties blend.

name: Ekofisk Field
note: Ekofisk was the first major Norwegian North Sea discovery and remains a long-life producing complex.

name: Troll Field
note: Troll is a giant Norwegian gas and oil field producing crude from a thin oil rim beneath a huge gas cap.

name: Athabasca Oil Sands
note: The Athabasca oil sands near Fort McMurray are the world's largest source of mined and in-situ bitumen.

name: Hibernia Field
note: Hibernia is an offshore field on the Grand Banks of Newfoundland produced via a gravity-base platform.

name: Nahr Bin Umar Field
note: Nahr Bin Umar is a southern Iraqi field feeding the Basra crude export stream.

name: Duri Field
note: Duri in central Sumatra is a giant heavy-oil field developed with one of the world's largest steamfloods.

name: Minas Field
note: Minas in central Sumatra is Indonesia's flagship field and source of the light Sumatran Light crude.

name: Mangala Field (Rajasthan)
note: Mangala in Rajasthan is India's largest onshore oil discovery, exported via a heated pipeline to Gujarat.

name: Mumbai High Field
note: Mumbai High is India's largest oil field, an offshore giant in the Arabian Sea operated by ONGC.

name: Bab Field
note: Onshore Abu Dhabi field anchoring the benchmark Murban crude stream.

name: West Qurna Field
note: Supergiant southern Iraqi field complex split between West Qurna-1 and West Qurna-2.

name: Tupi (Lula) Field
note: Flagship Brazilian pre-salt field in the Santos Basin and the country's largest producer.

name: Buzios Field
note: Giant pre-salt field offshore Brazil, the largest deepwater field by output growth.

name: Liza / Payara (Stabroek)
note: Producing FPSO developments within the prolific Stabroek block offshore Guyana.

name: El Sharara Field
note: Libya's largest oilfield in the Murzuq Basin, frequently affected by blockades.

name: Azeri-Chirag-Gunashli (ACG)
note: BP-operated Caspian field complex feeding the BTC pipeline to the Mediterranean.

name: Dukhan Field
note: Qatar's main onshore oilfield running along the western side of the peninsula.

name: Sarir Field
note: Large field in eastern Libya's Sirte Basin operated by AGOCO.

name: Block 0 / Malongo (Cabinda)
note: Long-producing shallow-water concession off Cabinda operated from the Malongo terminal.

name: Sakhalin-1 (Chayvo)
note: Offshore oil project off northeast Sakhalin Island exporting Sokol crude to Asia.

name: El Furrial Field
note: Light-crude field in eastern Venezuela's Monagas state north of the Orinoco Belt.

name: Vadinar Refinery (Nayara)
note: Large west-coast Indian refinery at Vadinar partly owned by Rosneft-led investors.

name: Mailiao Refinery (Formosa)
note: Taiwan's largest refinery and a major Asian fuels exporter at Mailiao.

name: Baytown Refinery (ExxonMobil)
note: One of the largest US refineries, on the Houston Ship Channel in Texas.

name: Baton Rouge Refinery (ExxonMobil)
note: Major Mississippi River refinery and petrochemical complex in Louisiana.

name: Garyville Refinery (Marathon)
note: One of the largest US refineries, on the Mississippi River in Garyville, Louisiana.

name: Whiting Refinery (BP)
note: Largest Midwest US refinery, configured to process Canadian heavy crude on Lake Michigan.

name: Lake Charles Refinery (Citgo)
note: Large heavy-crude refinery on the Calcasieu River in southwest Louisiana.

name: Beaumont Refinery (ExxonMobil)
note: Expanded southeast Texas refinery that became one of the largest in the US.

name: Los Angeles Refinery (Marathon)
note: Largest West Coast US refinery, spanning Carson and Wilmington in Los Angeles.

name: Jubail Refinery (SATORP)
note: Aramco-Total joint-venture refinery on the Saudi Gulf coast at Jubail.

name: Yanbu Refinery (YASREF)
note: Aramco-Sinopec full-conversion refinery on the Saudi Red Sea coast.

name: Abadan Refinery
operator: National Iranian Oil Refining & Distribution Co.
note: Historic and one of Iran's largest refineries on the Shatt al-Arab at Abadan.

name: Sitra Refinery (Bapco)
note: Bahrain's sole refinery at Sitra, recently expanded under the BAPCO modernization program.

name: Mina Al-Ahmadi Refinery (KNPC)
note: Major Kuwaiti coastal refinery integrated with the Mina Al-Ahmadi export terminal.

name: Al-Zour Refinery (KIPIC)
note: Kuwait's newest mega-refinery at Al-Zour, among the largest single refineries worldwide.

name: Sohar Refinery (OQ)
note: Coastal Omani refinery at the Sohar industrial port outside the Strait of Hormuz.

name: Duqm Refinery (OQ8)
note: New Oman-Kuwait joint-venture refinery on the Arabian Sea coast at Duqm.

name: Rabigh Refinery (Petro Rabigh)
note: Integrated refining and petrochemical complex on the Saudi Red Sea coast at Rabigh.

name: Jamnagar SEZ Refinery (Reliance)
note: Export-oriented SEZ refinery adjacent to Reliance's domestic Jamnagar unit, forming the world's largest refining hub.

name: Paradip Refinery (IOC)
note: Coastal east-Indian refinery configured for heavy and high-sulphur crude at Paradip.

name: Mangalore Refinery (MRPL)
note: West-coast Indian refinery at Mangalore operated by an ONGC subsidiary.

name: Zhenhai Refinery (Sinopec)
note: One of China's largest refineries, integrated with petrochemicals near Ningbo.

name: Zhoushan Refinery (Rongsheng/ZPC)
note: Privately built mega-refinery and petrochemical base on Zhoushan island, among the world's largest.

name: Dalian Refinery (PetroChina)
note: Large northeast Chinese coastal refinery handling imported crude at Dalian.

name: Maoming Refinery (Sinopec)
note: Major refining and petrochemical complex in southern Guangdong province.

name: Yeosu Refinery (GS Caltex)
note: One of the world's largest single refineries, on the South Korean south coast at Yeosu.

name: Onsan Refinery (S-Oil)
note: Aramco-controlled refinery and petrochemical complex at Onsan near Ulsan.

name: Kawasaki Refinery (ENEOS)
note: Large refinery on Tokyo Bay serving the Japanese capital region.

name: Map Ta Phut Refinery (PTTGC/IRPC)
note: Integrated refining and petrochemical hub at Map Ta Phut on Thailand's eastern seaboard.

name: Cilacap Refinery (Pertamina)
note: Indonesia's largest refinery, on the south coast of Central Java at Cilacap.

name: Melaka Refinery (Petronas)
note: Petronas refining complex on the Strait of Malacca at Melaka.

name: Pengerang Refinery (PRefChem/RAPID)
note: Integrated refinery and petrochemical project at Pengerang in southern Johor.

name: Jose / Cardon Refining Complex
note: Orinoco crude upgraders and export terminals at Jose, operating well below nameplate capacity.

name: Cadereyta Refinery (Pemex)
note: Inland Mexican refinery serving the industrial northeast near Monterrey.

name: Olmeca Refinery (Dos Bocas)
note: Mexico's newest refinery on the Gulf coast at Dos Bocas in Tabasco.

name: Replan Refinery (Paulinia)
note: Brazil's largest refinery, located inland at Paulinia in Sao Paulo state.

name: Karlsruhe Refinery (MiRO)
note: Germany's largest refinery, on the Rhine at Karlsruhe.

name: Antwerp Refinery (TotalEnergies)
note: One of Europe's largest refineries and petrochemical platforms in the Port of Antwerp.

name: Sines Refinery (Galp)
note: Portugal's largest refinery on the Atlantic coast at the deepwater port of Sines.

name: Fawley Refinery (ExxonMobil)
note: The UK's largest refinery, on Southampton Water on the south English coast.

name: Gdansk Refinery (Orlen Lotos)
note: Poland's coastal refinery at Gdansk supplied via the Naftoport crude terminal.

name: Plock Refinery (Orlen)
note: Poland's largest refinery and petrochemical hub at Plock on the Vistula.

name: Skikda Refinery (Sonatrach)
note: Algeria's largest refinery on the Mediterranean coast at Skikda.

name: Port Harcourt Refinery (NNPC)
note: State-owned Niger Delta refinery undergoing rehabilitation to restore output.

name: Dangote Refinery
note: Africa's largest refinery at Lekki near Lagos, designed to process Nigerian and imported crude.

name: Durban Refinery (SAPREF)
note: South Africa's largest refinery at Durban, idled and slated for restart under new ownership.

name: Sarnia Refining Complex
note: Cluster of refineries at Sarnia, Ontario, processing crude piped from western Canada.

name: Edmonton / Strathcona Refinery
note: Refinery in Alberta's Edmonton industrial belt processing local synthetic and conventional crude.

name: Sriracha Refinery (Thai Oil)
note: Thailand's largest refinery on the Gulf coast at Sriracha near Laem Chabang.

name: Ras Tanura Crude Terminal
note: World's largest crude oil export terminal, on the Saudi Gulf coast at Ras Tanura.

name: Basra Oil Terminal
note: Iraq's principal crude export outlet via offshore terminals at the head of the Gulf.

name: Kharg Island Oil Terminal
note: Iran's main crude export terminal on Kharg Island in the northern Persian Gulf.

name: Fujairah Oil Terminal
note: Strategic oil storage, bunkering, and export hub outside the Strait of Hormuz at Fujairah.

name: Ceyhan Oil Terminal (BTC)
note: Mediterranean export terminus of the Baku-Tbilisi-Ceyhan and Kirkuk-Ceyhan pipelines.

name: Louisiana Offshore Oil Port (LOOP)
note: The only US deepwater port able to load VLCCs, offshore from Louisiana in the Gulf of Mexico.

name: Corpus Christi Crude Export Hub
note: Leading US crude export gateway shipping Permian shale oil from the Texas Gulf coast.

name: Primorsk Oil Terminal
note: Russia's main Baltic crude export terminal at Primorsk near St. Petersburg.

name: Kozmino Oil Terminal (ESPO)
note: Pacific terminus of the ESPO pipeline exporting Russian crude to Asia.

name: Dolphin Gas Project (Khuff)
note: Dolphin draws Khuff gas from Qatar's North Field for pipeline export to the UAE and Oman.

name: Plaquemines LNG Terminal
note: Plaquemines LNG on the Mississippi River began producing LNG in late 2024 in Louisiana.

name: Calcasieu Pass LNG Terminal
note: Calcasieu Pass LNG in Cameron Parish, Louisiana, exports LNG using modular liquefaction trains.

name: Cove Point LNG Terminal
note: Cove Point LNG in Maryland is one of the few US LNG export terminals on the Atlantic coast.

name: Elba Island LNG Terminal
note: Elba Island LNG near Savannah, Georgia, uses small modular liquefaction units for export.

name: Golden Pass LNG Terminal
note: Golden Pass LNG at Sabine Pass, Texas, is a joint QatarEnergy-ExxonMobil export project nearing startup.

name: Hammerfest LNG (Snohvit)
note: Hammerfest LNG on Melkoya island liquefies gas from the offshore Snohvit field in the Barents Sea.

name: Troll Gas Field
note: Troll is Norway's largest gas field and a cornerstone of European pipeline gas supply.

name: Ormen Lange Gas Field
note: Ormen Lange is a deepwater gas field feeding the Langeled pipeline to the United Kingdom.

name: Hassi R'Mel Gas Field
note: Hassi R'Mel is Algeria's giant gas field and the hub feeding trans-Mediterranean export pipelines.

name: Arzew LNG Complex
note: Arzew hosts Algeria's historic and modern LNG trains exporting to southern Europe.

name: Skikda LNG Complex
note: Skikda LNG on Algeria's eastern coast liquefies Saharan gas for Mediterranean export.

name: South Pars Gas Field (Iran)
note: South Pars is the Iranian portion of the world's largest gas field shared with Qatar's North Field.

name: Kish Gas Field
note: Kish is a large independent Iranian gas field under phased development in the Persian Gulf.

name: Galkynysh Gas Field
note: Galkynysh holds vast reserves and anchors Turkmen pipeline exports to China.

name: Dauletabad Gas Field
note: Dauletabad supplies gas to pipelines running toward Iran and the Central Asia-China line.

name: Urengoy Gas Field
note: Urengoy in the Nadym-Pur-Taz region is one of the largest gas fields ever developed.

name: Yamburg Gas Field
note: Yamburg on the Taz Peninsula is a core Gazprom supergiant feeding the Russian export grid.

name: Sakhalin-2 LNG (Prigorodnoye)
note: Sakhalin-2 at Prigorodnoye is Russia's main Pacific LNG plant serving Asian buyers.

name: Portovaya LNG Terminal
note: Portovaya LNG near Vyborg liquefies gas at the Nord Stream entry point on the Baltic.

name: Ichthys Gas Field
note: Ichthys is a major offshore gas-condensate field piping to the Darwin LNG-area Bladin Point plant.

name: Queensland Curtis LNG (QCLNG)
note: QCLNG on Curtis Island liquefies coal-seam gas from Queensland's Surat-Bowen basins.

name: Pluto LNG Terminal
note: Pluto LNG near Karratha liquefies gas from offshore Carnarvon Basin fields.

name: Mozambique LNG (Area 1, Afungi)
note: Mozambique LNG on the Afungi peninsula will liquefy Rovuma Basin Area 1 gas for export.

name: Coral Sul FLNG
note: Coral Sul is a floating LNG facility on the offshore Coral gas field in Mozambique's Area 4.

name: Zohr Gas Field
note: Zohr is the largest gas discovery in the Mediterranean and anchors Egyptian domestic supply.

name: Idku LNG Terminal
note: Idku (ELNG) near Alexandria liquefies Egyptian Mediterranean gas for export when surplus allows.

name: Damietta LNG Terminal
note: Damietta LNG on Egypt's Mediterranean coast exports gas processed from Nile Delta and offshore fields.

name: Bonny Island LNG (NLNG)
note: Bonny Island NLNG is Nigeria's main LNG export complex fed by Niger Delta associated and non-associated gas.

name: Greater Tortue Ahmeyim FLNG
note: Greater Tortue Ahmeyim is a floating LNG project straddling the Mauritania-Senegal maritime border.

name: Tangguh LNG Terminal
note: Tangguh LNG in Bintuni Bay liquefies gas from offshore Berau fields in West Papua.

name: Bontang LNG Terminal
note: Bontang in East Kalimantan was long Indonesia's flagship LNG complex fed by Mahakam Delta gas.

name: PFLNG Satu / Dua (Malaysia FLNG)
note: Petronas operates floating LNG units offshore Malaysia to monetise remote gas fields.

name: Leviathan Gas Field
note: Leviathan is the largest Eastern Mediterranean gas field, supplying Israel, Egypt and Jordan.

name: Tamar Gas Field
note: Tamar offshore Israel was the country's first major deepwater gas field and underpins domestic power.

name: Shah Deniz Gas Field
note: Shah Deniz in the Caspian feeds the Southern Gas Corridor pipeline system to Turkey and Europe.

name: Karachaganak Gas-Condensate Field
note: Karachaganak is a giant gas-condensate field in northwest Kazakhstan exporting via Russia and CPC.

name: Hugoton Gas Field
note: Hugoton spanning Kansas-Oklahoma-Texas is one of North America's largest conventional gas fields.

name: Barnett Shale
note: The Barnett Shale near Fort Worth was the first commercial shale gas play to scale via fracking.

name: Utica Shale (Ohio)
note: The Utica Shale beneath eastern Ohio is a prolific deep dry-gas play in the Appalachian Basin.

name: Anadarko Basin (SCOOP/STACK)
note: The Anadarko Basin SCOOP and STACK plays in Oklahoma yield significant associated and dry gas.

name: Groningen Slochteren (legacy)
note: The Slochteren reservoir of the Groningen field was permanently shut to halt induced seismicity.

name: Bahr Essalam Gas Field
note: Bahr Essalam offshore Libya supplies the Greenstream pipeline carrying gas to Sicily.

name: Yadana Gas Field
note: Yadana offshore Myanmar pipes gas to Thailand and remains a key Myanmar export earner.

name: Camisea Gas Field (Block 88)
capacity: anchors Peru LNG and domestic gas
note: Camisea in the Peruvian Amazon supplies domestic gas and the Pampa Melchorita LNG plant.

name: Peru LNG (Pampa Melchorita)
note: Peru LNG at Pampa Melchorita is South America's only operating LNG export plant, fed by Camisea gas.

name: LNG Canada (Kitimat)
note: LNG Canada at Kitimat is Canada's first large-scale LNG export terminal on the Pacific coast.

name: Montney Formation
note: The Montney straddling BC and Alberta is Canada's most prolific tight gas and liquids play.

name: North Rankin Gas Field
capacity: core North West Shelf feed gas
note: North Rankin offshore Western Australia is a foundation gas field for the North West Shelf project.

name: Corpus Christi Stage 3 LNG
note: Corpus Christi Stage 3 adds mid-scale liquefaction trains alongside Cheniere's existing Texas terminal.

name: Karish Gas Field
note: Karish offshore Israel is produced through a floating production unit supplying the domestic market.

name: Ruwais LNG (Al Ruwais)
note: Ruwais LNG in Abu Dhabi will roughly double UAE liquefaction capacity using electrified trains.

name: Das Island LNG
note: Das Island is the UAE's long-running offshore LNG export plant in the Persian Gulf.

name: North Field East Expansion
note: North Field East trains at Ras Laffan are expanding Qatar's LNG capacity toward 126 Mtpa.

name: Prudhoe Bay Gas Cap
note: Prudhoe Bay holds a giant gas cap on the Alaska North Slope, largely reinjected for oil recovery.

name: Sui Gas Field
note: Sui in Balochistan was the foundational gas field of Pakistan's domestic energy network.

name: Krishna-Godavari Basin (KG-D6)
note: The KG-D6 deepwater fields off Andhra Pradesh are India's most important domestic gas source.

name: Kharasavey Gas Field
note: Kharasavey on the Yamal Peninsula is a Gazprom field developed to sustain Arctic gas output.

name: Radomiro Tomic Mine
note: Codelco open-pit oxide and sulphide copper mine in the Calama district of northern Chile.

name: Ministro Hales Mine
note: Codelco open-pit copper mine near Calama with high arsenic concentrates.

name: Gabriela Mistral Mine
note: Codelco SX-EW cathode operation south of Calama in the Atacama Desert.

name: Salvador Mine
note: Historic Codelco copper division in the Atacama region undergoing the Rajo Inca expansion.

name: Andina Mine
note: Codelco high-altitude copper mine in the central Andes north of Santiago.

name: Los Pelambres Mine
note: Antofagasta Minerals porphyry copper-molybdenum mine in the Choapa province.

name: Centinela Mine
note: Antofagasta Minerals copper-gold operation combining concentrator and SX-EW cathode plants.

name: Candelaria Mine
note: Lundin Mining copper-gold mine near Copiapo with open-pit and underground operations.

name: El Abra Mine
note: Freeport-Codelco joint-venture SX-EW copper cathode operation northeast of Calama.

name: Quebrada Blanca Mine
note: Teck high-altitude porphyry copper mine expanded under the QB2 project in northern Chile.

name: Mantos Blancos Mine
note: Capstone Copper open-pit copper mine east of Antofagasta producing concentrate and cathode.

name: Toquepala Mine
note: Southern Copper open-pit porphyry copper mine in the southern Peruvian Andes.

name: Cuajone Mine
note: Southern Copper open-pit copper mine in the Moquegua region of southern Peru.

name: Toromocho Mine
note: Chinalco high-altitude porphyry copper mine in the central Peruvian Andes at Morococha.

name: Constancia Mine
note: Hudbay Minerals open-pit copper-molybdenum mine in the Chumbivilcas province of southern Peru.

name: Antapaccay Mine
note: Glencore open-pit copper-gold mine in the Espinar province of the Cusco region.

name: Quellaveco Mine
note: Anglo American large open-pit porphyry copper mine in the Moquegua region that began production in 2022.

name: Mirador Mine
note: Ecuador's first large-scale open-pit copper mine in the southeastern Cordillera del Condor.

name: Cobre Panama Mine
note: First Quantum large open-pit copper mine suspended in 2023 after a Panamanian court ruling.

name: Pinos Altos / La Caridad Mine
note: Southern Copper La Caridad open-pit copper mine and smelter complex in Sonora, Mexico.

name: Safford Mine
note: Freeport-McMoRan SX-EW copper operation including the Lone Star expansion in eastern Arizona.

name: Sierrita Mine
note: Freeport-McMoRan open-pit copper-molybdenum mine south of Tucson, Arizona.

name: Bagdad Mine
note: Freeport-McMoRan open-pit copper-molybdenum mine in northwestern Arizona.

name: Ray Mine
note: Asarco open-pit copper mine and SX-EW operation in the Pinal County copper district of Arizona.

name: Chino Mine
note: Freeport-McMoRan open-pit copper mine at Santa Rita, one of the oldest active mines in the United States.

name: Olympic Dam (copper)
note: BHP underground polymetallic mine producing copper, uranium, gold and silver in South Australia.

name: Cadia Valley Operations
note: Newmont gold-copper block-cave mine near Orange in central New South Wales.

name: Mount Isa Copper Operations
note: Glencore underground copper mining and smelting complex at Mount Isa in northwest Queensland.

name: Prominent Hill Mine
note: Underground copper-gold mine in the Gawler Craton of South Australia acquired by BHP via OZ Minerals.

name: Udokan Mine
note: Large copper deposit in eastern Siberia developed by Udokan Copper, with first production in 2023.

name: Gaysky GOK
note: UMMC underground copper-zinc mine in the southern Urals, Russia's largest copper ore producer.

name: Aktogay Mine
note: KAZ Minerals large open-pit porphyry copper mine in eastern Kazakhstan.

name: Bozshakol Mine
note: KAZ Minerals open-pit copper mine in the Pavlodar region of northern Kazakhstan.

name: Erdenet Mine
note: Large open-pit copper-molybdenum mine in northern Mongolia, one of Asia's largest.

name: Lubin Mine (KGHM)
note: KGHM deep underground copper-silver mine in the Legnica-Glogow copper belt of southwest Poland.

name: Rudna Mine (KGHM)
note: KGHM's largest underground copper mine in the Lubin-Glogow district of Poland.

name: Bor Copper Mine
note: Zijin Mining copper mining and smelting complex in eastern Serbia formerly run by RTB Bor.

name: Cukaru Peki Mine
note: Zijin Mining high-grade copper-gold deposit at Timok in eastern Serbia in early production.

name: Mes Aynak Deposit
note: One of the world's largest undeveloped copper deposits, south of Kabul, held by a Chinese consortium.

name: Reko Diq Deposit
note: Large undeveloped copper-gold porphyry deposit in southwestern Pakistan being advanced by Barrick.

name: Kakula Underground Mine
note: High-grade underground copper mine within the Kamoa-Kakula complex in the DRC's Central African Copperbelt.

name: Kinsevere Mine
note: MMG open-pit copper mine and SX-EW plant near Lubumbashi in the DRC Copperbelt.

name: Kamoto Copper Company (KCC copper)
note: Glencore large copper-cobalt mining complex at Kolwezi in the DRC Copperbelt.

name: Metalkol RTR
note: ERG copper-cobalt tailings reprocessing operation at Kolwezi in the DRC.

name: Lumwana Mine
note: Barrick open-pit copper mine in northwestern Zambia targeted for a major super-pit expansion.

name: Mopani Copper Mines
note: Underground copper mining and smelting complex at Mufulira and Kitwe in the Zambian Copperbelt.

name: Kalumbila (Trident) Mine
note: First Quantum Sentinel-Kalumbila copper operation in the new North-Western copper district of Zambia.

name: Jinchuan Group Copper Smelter
note: Jinchuan Group integrated copper and nickel smelter-refinery in Jinchang, Gansu province.

name: Tongling Jinchang Copper Smelter
note: Tongling Nonferrous large copper smelting and refining complex on the Yangtze in Anhui province.

name: Daye Nonferrous Copper Smelter
note: Daye Nonferrous copper smelter and refinery at Huangshi in Hubei province, China.

name: Yantai Penghui (Yanggu Xiangguang) Copper Smelter
note: Yanggu Xiangguang flash-smelting copper refinery in Liaocheng, Shandong province.

name: Birla Copper Smelter (Dahej)
note: Hindalco Birla Copper smelter and refinery at Dahej in Gujarat, India's largest single-location copper plant.

name: Onsan Copper Smelter (LS-Nikko)
note: LS MnM (formerly LS-Nikko) copper smelter and refinery at Onsan, one of the world's largest single-site smelters.

name: Toyo Copper Smelter (Sumitomo)
note: Sumitomo Metal Mining flash smelter at Saijo on Shikoku, a major Japanese copper producer.

name: Manyar Copper Smelter (Gresik)
note: Freeport Indonesia single-line copper smelter at Manyar in Gresik processing Grasberg concentrate.

name: PASAR Copper Smelter (Isabel)
note: Philippine Associated Smelting and Refining copper smelter at Isabel, Leyte.

name: Glogow Copper Smelter (KGHM)
note: KGHM flash and shaft copper smelter-refinery at Glogow in the Polish copper belt.

name: Ilo Copper Smelter (Southern Copper)
note: Southern Copper smelter and refinery on the Pacific coast at Ilo, southern Peru.

name: La Caridad Copper Smelter (Sonora)
note: Grupo Mexico integrated copper smelter and refinery at Nacozari in Sonora, Mexico.

name: Sterlite Tuticorin Copper Smelter
note: Vedanta Sterlite copper smelter at Thoothukudi closed since 2018 following environmental protests.

name: Caraiba Copper Smelter (Dias d'Avila)
note: Paranapanema copper smelter and refinery at Dias d'Avila in Bahia, Brazil's main copper plant.

name: Ronnskar Copper Smelter (Boliden)
note: Boliden copper smelter at Skelleftehamn that also recovers metals from electronic scrap.

name: Salobo Mine
note: Vale large open-pit copper-gold mine in the Carajas mineral province of northern Brazil.

name: Sossego Mine
note: Vale open-pit copper-gold mine in the Carajas province of Para state, Brazil.

name: Kevitsa Mine
note: Boliden open-pit nickel-copper mine in Sodankyla, Finnish Lapland.

name: Kitimat Aluminium Smelter
note: Hydro-powered aluminium smelter at Kitimat on the British Columbia coast.

name: Alma Aluminium Smelter
note: Quebec hydropower aluminium smelter on the Saguenay River.

name: Becancour Aluminium Smelter (ABI)
note: Quebec aluminium smelter on the south shore of the St Lawrence at Becancour.

name: Baie-Comeau Aluminium Smelter
note: Alcoa aluminium smelter on Quebec's North Shore powered by regional hydropower.

name: Bratsk Aluminium Smelter
note: One of the world's largest aluminium smelters, at Bratsk in Siberia on cheap hydropower.

name: Krasnoyarsk Aluminium Smelter (KrAZ)
note: Major Siberian aluminium smelter at Krasnoyarsk fed by Yenisei hydropower.

name: Sayanogorsk Aluminium Smelter
note: Siberian aluminium smelter powered by the Sayano-Shushenskaya hydroelectric plant.

name: Hindalco Aditya Aluminium Smelter
note: Hindalco greenfield aluminium smelter in Odisha with captive coal power.

name: Vedanta Jharsuguda Aluminium Smelter
note: India's largest aluminium smelter, at Jharsuguda in Odisha with captive power.

name: NALCO Angul Aluminium Smelter
note: State-owned Indian aluminium smelter at Angul with captive coal-fired power.

name: Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) Smelter
note: One of the world's largest single-site aluminium smelters, on Bahrain's coast at Askar.

name: EGA Jebel Ali (DUBAL) Aluminium Smelter
note: EGA's Jebel Ali aluminium smelter in Dubai, a core part of the UAE primary-metal industry.

name: Qatalum Aluminium Smelter (Mesaieed)
note: Gas-powered aluminium smelter at Mesaieed jointly owned by Hydro and Qatar Energy.

name: Sohar Aluminium Smelter
note: Gas-powered aluminium smelter at Sohar on Oman's Batinah coast.

name: Ma'aden Aluminium Smelter (Ras Al Khair)
note: Integrated alumina-aluminium complex at Ras Al Khair on Saudi Arabia's Gulf coast.

name: Hydro Sunndal Aluminium Smelter
note: Norway's largest aluminium smelter, at Sunndalsora, powered by hydropower.

name: Hydro Ardal Aluminium Smelter
note: Hydropower aluminium smelter at Ardalstangen on Norway's western fjords.

name: Husnes Aluminium Smelter (Soral)
note: Hydropower aluminium smelter at Husnes in western Norway.

name: Fjardaal Aluminium Smelter (Reydarfjordur)
note: Alcoa aluminium smelter in eastern Iceland powered by Karahnjukar hydropower.

name: Grundartangi Aluminium Smelter
note: Century Aluminum smelter at Grundartangi using Icelandic geothermal and hydropower.

name: Mozal Aluminium Smelter
note: Large aluminium smelter near Maputo powered by hydropower imported from Mozambique and South Africa.

name: Hillside Aluminium Smelter (Richards Bay)
note: Africa's largest aluminium smelter, at Richards Bay on South Africa's east coast.

name: Dunkerque Aluminium Smelter
note: Largest primary aluminium smelter in the European Union, on the coast at Dunkirk.

name: Sept-Iles (Alouette) Aluminium Smelter
note: Largest aluminium smelter in the Americas, at Sept-Iles in Quebec on hydropower.

name: Slovalco Aluminium Smelter (Ziar nad Hronom)
note: Central European aluminium smelter at Ziar nad Hronom, output curtailed by high power prices.

name: Xinjiang East Hopetone / Tianshan Aluminium Cluster
note: Coal-powered aluminium smelting cluster in Xinjiang, a major hub of Chinese primary output.

name: Yunnan Hongta / Yunlv Aluminium Cluster
note: Hydropower-based aluminium smelting cluster in Yunnan attracting capacity relocated for green power.

name: Inner Mongolia Aluminium Smelter Cluster (Baotou)
note: Large coal-powered aluminium smelting cluster around Baotou in Inner Mongolia.

name: Asahan (Inalum) Aluminium Smelter
note: Indonesia's main aluminium smelter at Kuala Tanjung powered by the Asahan River hydropower.

name: Tomago Aluminium Smelter
note: Australia's largest aluminium smelter, near Newcastle in New South Wales.

name: Boyne Island Aluminium Smelter, Gladstone
note: Aluminium smelter at Boyne Island near Gladstone, integrated with Queensland alumina supply.

name: TSMC Fab 12 (N5/N7) - Hsinchu
note: TSMC Fab 12 in the Hsinchu Science Park runs leading-edge 5nm and 7nm logic nodes.

name: TSMC Fab 14 - Tainan
note: TSMC Fab 14 in the Southern Taiwan Science Park produces mature and mid-range logic nodes.

name: TSMC Fab 15 - Taichung
note: TSMC Fab 15 in the Central Taiwan Science Park is a major advanced-logic gigafab.

name: TSMC JASM Kumamoto Fab
note: TSMC JASM fab in Kikuyo, Kumamoto serves automotive and image-sensor chip demand in Japan.

name: Samsung Giheung Campus
note: Samsung's Giheung campus is the company's original semiconductor site hosting foundry and R&D lines.

name: Samsung Taylor Fab (Texas)
note: Samsung's Taylor, Texas fab is being built to produce advanced sub-5nm logic chips.

name: SK Hynix Cheongju (M15) Campus
note: SK Hynix's Cheongju campus including the M15 fab is a major NAND flash production hub.

name: Intel Fab 52 (Ocotillo expansion)
note: Intel's new Fab 52 at the Ocotillo, Chandler site targets Intel 18A leading-edge process technology.

name: Intel Fab 9/11x - Rio Rancho
note: Intel's Rio Rancho, New Mexico site has been converted to advanced Foveros 3D packaging.

name: Intel Fab 34 - Leixlip
note: Intel's Fab 34 in Leixlip is Europe's first high-volume EUV-based logic fab.

name: GlobalFoundries Fab 8 - Malta NY
note: GlobalFoundries Fab 8 in Malta, New York is the company's most advanced FinFET facility.

name: Texas Instruments Sherman Fabs
note: Texas Instruments is building a multi-fab 300mm analog chip complex in Sherman, Texas.

name: Micron Manassas Fab
note: Micron's Manassas, Virginia fab produces long-lifecycle memory for automotive and industrial uses.

name: Micron Taichung Fab
note: Micron's Taichung fabs in central Taiwan are a core site for the company's advanced DRAM output.

name: SMIC Shanghai Fab (Zhangjiang)
note: SMIC's Shanghai Zhangjiang fabs are China's most advanced domestic foundry lines.

name: SMIC Beijing Fab
note: SMIC's Beijing fabs in the Yizhuang zone produce large volumes of mature-node logic chips.

name: Hua Hong Wuxi Fab
note: Hua Hong's Wuxi 300mm fab focuses on mature analog, power and embedded memory devices.

name: YMTC Wuhan Fab
note: YMTC's Wuhan campus is China's leading 3D NAND flash memory fabrication site.

name: CXMT Hefei Fab
note: CXMT's Hefei fab is China's primary domestic DRAM memory producer.

name: Kioxia Yokkaichi Fab
note: The Kioxia-Western Digital Yokkaichi site is one of the world's largest NAND flash fabs.

name: Kioxia Kitakami Fab
note: Kioxia's Kitakami fab in Iwate is a newer NAND flash site complementing Yokkaichi.

name: Sony Nagasaki Image Sensor Fab
note: Sony's Nagasaki fab is the leading global producer of CMOS image sensors for smartphones.

name: Renesas Naka Fab
note: Renesas' Naka fab in Ibaraki is a key 300mm site for automotive microcontrollers.

name: Infineon Dresden Smart Power Fab
note: Infineon's Dresden site is expanding with a major new 300mm power and analog megafab.

name: STMicroelectronics Crolles Fab
note: ST's Crolles site near Grenoble runs advanced FD-SOI and mixed-signal 300mm logic.

name: GlobalFoundries Singapore Fab 7
note: GlobalFoundries' Woodlands Fab 7 in Singapore makes specialty RF, power and embedded chips.

name: Intel Fab 28 - Kiryat Gat
note: Intel's Fab 28 in Kiryat Gat is a major advanced-logic site outside the United States.

name: Tower Semiconductor Migdal HaEmek Fab
note: Tower Semiconductor's Migdal HaEmek site produces specialty analog and RF foundry wafers.

name: Port of Shenzhen (Yantian)
note: Shenzhen's Yantian and western terminals form one of the world's busiest container port complexes.

name: Port of Qingdao
note: Qingdao is a leading northern Chinese container, ore and crude oil gateway port.

name: Port of Xiamen
note: Xiamen is a major container port serving China's southeastern Fujian export economy.

name: Port of Hong Kong (Kwai Tsing)
note: Hong Kong's Kwai Tsing terminals remain a major transshipment hub for the Pearl River Delta.

name: Port of Tanjung Pelepas
note: Tanjung Pelepas in Johor is a fast-growing Strait of Malacca transshipment container hub.

name: Port of Laem Chabang
note: Laem Chabang is Thailand's main deep-sea container gateway serving Bangkok's industrial belt.

name: Port of Tanjung Priok (Jakarta)
note: Tanjung Priok in Jakarta is Indonesia's largest and busiest container port.

name: Port of Nhava Sheva (JNPT)
note: Jawaharlal Nehru Port near Mumbai is India's largest container handling facility.

name: Port of Mundra
note: Mundra in Gujarat is India's largest commercial port by total cargo throughput.

name: Port of Salalah
note: Salalah is a major Arabian Sea transshipment hub on the Asia-Europe shipping lane.

name: Port of Tanger Med
note: Tanger Med on the Strait of Gibraltar is Africa's largest container transshipment port.

name: Port of Piraeus
note: COSCO-run Piraeus is the leading Mediterranean container gateway into southeastern Europe.

name: Port of Valencia
note: Valencia is the busiest container port in the western Mediterranean basin.

name: Port of Algeciras
note: Algeciras on the Strait of Gibraltar is a key Mediterranean transshipment and bunkering port.

name: Port of Hamburg
note: Hamburg is Germany's largest seaport and a primary Northern Range container gateway.

name: Port of Bremerhaven
note: Bremerhaven is a major German container and roll-on/roll-off vehicle export port.

name: Port of Felixstowe
note: Felixstowe is the United Kingdom's largest and busiest container port.

name: Port of Le Havre
note: Le Havre is France's leading deep-sea container port on the English Channel.

name: Port of Gioia Tauro
note: Gioia Tauro in Calabria is Italy's largest container transshipment hub in the central Mediterranean.

name: Port of New York and New Jersey
note: New York-New Jersey is the largest container port on the United States East Coast.

name: Port of Savannah
note: Savannah's Garden City Terminal is the largest single container terminal in North America.

name: Port of Manzanillo
note: Manzanillo on Mexico's Pacific coast is the country's busiest container port.

name: Port of Balboa (Panama Canal)
note: Balboa at the Pacific entrance of the Panama Canal is a key inter-ocean transshipment hub.

name: Port of Cartagena
note: Cartagena is the Caribbean's main container transshipment gateway in northern South America.

name: Port Said (Suez Canal)
note: Port Said at the Mediterranean mouth of the Suez Canal is a major East-West transshipment hub.

name: Port of Mombasa
note: Mombasa is East Africa's principal seaport serving Kenya and landlocked interior states.

name: Port of Lagos (Apapa / Tin Can)
note: The Apapa and Tin Can complexes in Lagos handle the bulk of Nigeria's container trade.

name: Port of Melbourne
note: Melbourne is Australia's largest container and general cargo seaport.

name: Port of Colon (MIT)
note: Colon on the Atlantic side of the Panama Canal is a leading Caribbean transshipment complex.

name: Port of Haifa
note: Haifa is one of Israel's main deep-water container and mixed-cargo ports on the Mediterranean.
